message:
perf: compact frozen codegen with shared runtime helpers — freeze all 10,828 functions

Replace duplicated inline cleanup/call/property-access patterns in generated
Zig with shared noinline runtime helpers, reducing generated code by ~40%.

Phase 1: Exception cleanup → exceptionCleanup() helper (~40% reduction)
- Every call/IC site emitted 5-14 lines of cleanup; now emits 1 line
- Both full codegen and relooper backends use the shared helper

Phase 2: Call wrappers → frozenCall/frozenCallMethod (~15-20% reduction)
- 15-25 line inline call blocks replaced with single helper call
- Handles CV stack extraction, closure sync, exception check

Phase 3: SP overflow check elimination (~5% reduction)
- Removed per-block sp overflow checks (stack sized from bytecode stack_size)

Phase 4: Property access → frozenGetField/frozenGetField2 (~5-10% reduction)
- get_field/get_field2 IC fallback paths wrapped in shared helpers

Phase 5: Value return cleanup → valueReturnCleanup() helper
- return/return_undef paths consolidated into single helper call

Phase 6: Conditional for-of declarations
- for_of_iter_stack/for_of_depth only emitted when function uses for-of

Phase 7: IC exception return fix for relooper
- getExceptionReturnExpr() provides correct cleanup in IC catch paths
- Fixed _var_ref_list const pointer mismatch (heap-alloc vs stack-alloc)

Also adds selective freezing support (--freeze-max-functions, --freeze-profile)
and PGO-guided function selection via call_profile.zig.

Results: 10,828/10,828 functions frozen, 91.7MB generated Zig (was ~143MB),
193MB binary, TSC runs in 1.17s (was 3.7s with N=200, 8.3s baseline).
